jobPostingInfo.title: Software Quality Engineer Intern - Summer 2026
jobPostingInfo.jobDescription:
Job Title: Software Quality Engineer Intern - Summer 2026

Job Category: Intern/Co-op

Time Type: Part time

Minimum Clearance Required to Start: None

Employee Type: Part-Time On-Call

Percentage of Travel Required: None

Type of Travel: None

* * *


The Opportunity:
Seeking a motivated Software Quality Engineer Intern to support the QA and Test Engineering team with improving and maintaining our automated test framework. This role will focus heavily on refactoring existing automated tests, troubleshooting and resolving broken tests, improving test reliability, and assisting with modernization of the automation codebase. The intern will work directly under the Test Team Lead to support ongoing test automation initiatives across integration, regression, and end-to-end testing efforts within a fast-paced Agile environment.

Responsibilities:

  • Refactor and reorganize existing automated test suites for maintainability and scalability
  • Investigate, troubleshoot, and repair failing or unstable automated tests
  • Assist with improving automation framework structure and reusable utilities
  • Update outdated test logic to align with current application functionality
  • Improve test execution stability and reduce flaky test behavior
  • Support regression testing activities across multiple environments
  • Assist with organizing test assets, scenarios, and reporting
  • Review test results and document findings/issues
  • Collaborate with developers, testers, and DevOps personnel to resolve automation-related issues
  • Participate in Agile ceremonies including standups, sprint planning, and backlog refinement
  • Help improve automation coverage and requirement traceability



Qualifications:

Required:

  • Currently pursuing a degree in: Computer Science Software Engineering Information Systems Computer Engineering Or related technical field
  • Basic understanding of software testing concepts
  • Familiarity with programming or scripting languages such as: TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Java
  • Basic understanding of Git/version control
  • Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills
  • Willingness to learn automation frameworks and CI/CD processes
  • Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship, now or at any time in the future


Desired:

  • Exposure to automated testing tools/frameworks such as: Playwright Cucumber Selenium
  • Familiarity with Agile development methodologies
  • Understanding of API testing concepts
  • Exposure to Linux, Docker, or Kubernetes environments
  • Experience working with Jira or similar tracking tools

-

What You Can Expect:

A culture of integrity.

At CACI, we place character and innovation at the center of everything we do. As a valued team member, you’ll be part of a high-performing group dedicated to our customer’s missions and driven by a higher purpose – to ensure the safety of our nation.

An environment of trust.

CACI values the unique contributions that every employee brings to our company and our customers - every day. You’ll have the autonomy to take the time you need through a unique flexible time off benefit and have access to robust learning resources to make your ambitions a reality.

A focus on continuous growth.

Together, we will advance our nation's most critical missions, build on our lengthy track record of business success, and find opportunities to break new ground — in your career and in our legacy.


Pay Range:

There are a host of factors that can influence final salary including, but not limited to, geographic location, Federal Government contract labor categories and contract wage rates, relevant prior work experience, specific skills and competencies, education, and certifications. Our employees value the flexibility at CACI that allows them to balance quality work and their personal lives. We offer competitive compensation, benefits and learning and development opportunities. Our broad and competitive mix of benefits options is designed to support and protect employees and their families. At CACI, you will receive comprehensive benefits such as; healthcare, wellness, financial, retirement, family support, continuing education, and time off benefits.

The proposed salary range for this position is:

$45,900 - $91,900

CACI is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, age, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other protected characteristic.
